original_title: Facilities Management & Workplace Services DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for facilities management and workplace services targeting lower value/less complex contracts and SME suppliers. It covers maintenance, repairs, installations, CAFM, help-desk services, and waste management for public sector estates across the UK.
what_they_want: Facilities management and workplace services including maintenance, response repairs, supply and installation works, CAFM (Computer-Aided Facilities Management), help-desk services, and waste management services. NOT medical devices.
eligibility_requirements: Open to suppliers (particularly targeting SMEs) who can provide facilities management services. Must be able to serve UK Public Sector Bodies including Central Government Departments, Local Authorities, Health, Police, Fire and Rescue, Education, Housing, Charities, Not for Profit, Nuclear, Devolved Administrations and International organizations.

original_title: Contract Notice
english_summary: The UK Health Security Agency is establishing a 4-year Dynamic Purchasing System (2024-2028) for COVID-19 vaccines to support future immunisation campaigns. The DPS has a potential total value of £1 billion (excluding VAT), with no minimum commitment, and will provide a flexible procurement route for approved suppliers to participate in future tenders.
what_they_want: COVID-19 vaccines and product(s) indicated for protection from COVID-19 for immunisation campaigns
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified suppliers who submit and have their DPS application approved through the Atamis eTendering system. Only suppliers accepted onto the DPS at the time an ITT (Invitation to Tender) is issued will be eligible to submit tenders.

original_title: P0819A - Application to join the Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the Domestic Homicide Reporting
english_summary: Birmingham City Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) to procure Independent Authors/Chairs to conduct statutory Domestic Homicide Reviews across the West Midlands region. The reviews examine deaths of persons aged 16+ resulting from domestic violence, abuse or neglect to identify lessons learned and prevent future homicides.
what_they_want: Independent Author/Chair services (combined role) to undertake statutory Domestic Homicide Reviews for deaths resulting from domestic violence, abuse or neglect across 7 West Midlands Local Authorities (Birmingham, Coventry, Dudley, Sandwell, Solihull, Walsall, and Wolverhampton).
evaluation_criteria: Two stage process - Stage 1 Selection Stage where potential suppliers must pass criteria to be appointed to the DPS; Stage 2 Award Stage where suppliers on the DPS will be invited to tender for specific contracts. Specific evaluation criteria and weightings not provided in this notice.

original_title: ASC23034 Dynamic Purchasing System for the Provision of Older People Residential and Nursing Care Long Term and Replacement Care Services
english_summary: Worcestershire County Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for older people residential and nursing care services across three lots covering long-term care, short-term replacement care, and emergency short-term replacement care. The DPS will run for four years with possible extensions up to eight years total, with an estimated lifetime value of £1,107,679,301 (excluding VAT).
what_they_want: Older people residential and nursing care services including: Lot 1 - Long Term Care, Lot 2 - Short Term Replacement Care, and Lot 3 - Emergency Short Term Replacement Care.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced, qualified and appropriately accredited suppliers. Applicants must complete the Standard Selection Questionnaire and upload relevant documents.

original_title: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS)
buyer_name: North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare NHS Trust
english_summary: North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare NHS Trust is reopening a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) covering North Staffordshire and Stoke on Trent. The DPS, which went live in February 2023, will run for 5 years and allows qualified organisations to bid on future CAMHS service requirements offering face-to-face, digital, and blended delivery approaches.
what_they_want: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) for ages 0-25 years (primarily 0-18 years) including multi-disciplinary team approaches, strengths-based and trauma informed care, outcome-focused support services, and various delivery modalities (face-to-face, digital, and blended approaches).
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced organisations with expertise in Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services. Organisations must indicate their area(s) of expertise and delivery modalities as part of the qualification process.

original_title: CHC Nursing Home Beds Preferred Provider List- NHS BLMK ICB
buyer_name: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton & Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board
english_summary: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board is seeking qualified providers of Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds to deliver safe and appropriate care in registered nursing home settings across the BLMK population. This is an Any Qualified Provider framework with zero value/volume contracts for 5 years with optional 2-year extension.
what_they_want: Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds services to be delivered in formal registered nursing home settings for clients assessed as requiring this level of care.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified providers of CHC nursing home beds services. Specific qualification requirements to be found in procurement documentation on Atamis project C153293.

original_title: CHC Nursing Home Beds Preferred Provider List- NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes ICB
english_summary: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board is seeking qualified providers of Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds to establish a preferred provider list. The service will provide safe and appropriate care in registered nursing home settings for clients assessed as needing this level of care across the BLMK population.
what_they_want: Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds and associated care services to be delivered in formal registered nursing home settings across Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified providers of CHC nursing home beds who meet Any Qualified Provider (AQP) criteria. Specific certification and experience requirements are referenced in procurement documentation available on Atamis platform.

original_title: Mental Health Recovery
english_summary: Rotherham Metropolitan Borough Council is seeking experienced providers to deliver community mental health care and support services through a Flexible Purchasing System (FPS). The services will focus on mental health recovery, personalized social care, and supporting independent living for people with mental ill-health in Rotherham.
what_they_want: Community mental health care and support services including supported living accommodation-based services and day opportunities. Services must be based on mental health recovery principles and provide personalized social care to help people achieve independent living skills.
eligibility_requirements: Experienced providers with capability and capacity to deliver community mental health care and support. Applicants must register with the Social Value Portal and commit to delivering Social Value additionality via all contracts.

original_title: DPS 4 - Supply of community equipment for small moving & handling equipment
english_summary: Croydon Council is procuring suppliers for a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) to provide community equipment to multiple local authorities through the Integrated Procurement Hub. This specific tender is for DPS 4, covering 32 categories of small moving and handling equipment.
what_they_want: Small moving and handling equipment across 32 categories of community equipment. Specific models or brands are not mentioned in this notice.
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet the DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ). Suppliers can apply at any time during the validity period and may resubmit if initially unsuccessful. No specific certifications, experience levels, or company size requirements are mentioned in this notice.

notice_id: Croydon Integrated Procurement Hub - DN179859 - DPS3
original_title: DPS 3 - Supply of community equipment for Simple Aids
english_summary: Croydon Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the procurement of simple aids community equipment on behalf of itself and partner local authorities through the Integrated Procurement Hub. This is DPS 3 covering 102 categories of simple aids equipment, operating as part of a shared service arrangement for community equipment procurement.
what_they_want: Simple aids community equipment across 102 categories. This is part of a broader DPS framework that also includes complex community equipment (31 categories in DPS 1) and paediatric community equipment (28 categories in DPS 2).
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ) to become an "Admitted Supplier". Suppliers can submit for evaluation at any time during the DPS validity period. Unsuccessful suppliers may re-submit revised PQQs at any time beyond the initial mini competition.

original_title: DPS 2 - Supply of community equipment paediatric and young adults
english_summary: Croydon Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of paediatric and young adult community equipment on behalf of itself and partner local authorities through the Integrated Procurement Hub. This is DPS_2 consisting of 28 categories of paediatric community equipment, operating alongside an existing DPS_1 for complex equipment.
what_they_want: 28 categories of paediatric and young adult community equipment (specific categories and model numbers not detailed in this notice)
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ). Unsuccessful suppliers may re-submit revised PQQs at any time. The DPS is open to new suppliers throughout its validity period.

original_title: Occupational Health and Related Services - DPS
english_summary: Crown Commercial Service is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for occupational health and related services for UK public sector and third sector organizations. The DPS will cover occupational health services, employee assistance programmes, fully managed services, eyecare services, and hearing services.
what_they_want: Occupational health services, employee assistance programmes, fully managed service, eyecare services, and hearing services.

original_title: Health Checks DPS
english_summary: This tender is for establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) to deliver NHS Health Checks for people aged 40-74 in West Northamptonshire to identify and manage cardiovascular disease risk factors. The program aims to offer health checks to 49,500 eligible people annually with a target uptake of 70% (34,650 checks per year) over a five-year period from April 2024 to February 2029.
what_they_want: NHS Health Check services for cardiovascular disease screening and risk factor assessment for eligible population aged 40-74 years with no previous history of CVD. Services must include early identification and management of cardiovascular risk factors.

original_title: Provision of Best Interest Assessors / Section 12 Doctors - Pseudo Open Framework
english_summary: Hull City Council is procuring Best Interest Assessors and Section 12 Doctors through a pseudo open framework agreement under the Light Touch Regime. The framework runs from April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2028 with an optional 24-month extension, with an estimated value of £200,000 per annum.
what_they_want: Lot 1 - Best Interest Assessors; Lot 2 - Section 12 Doctors. These are professional services, not medical devices or equipment.

original_title: Functional Electrical Stimulation Supplies and Maintenance
buyer_name: Procurement Assist Limited c/o Integrated Facilities Management Bolton Ltd (iFM Bolton)
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply and maintenance of Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) equipment and supplies to UK public healthcare providers and other public sector organizations. The DPS has been extended to run until 31 May 2029.
what_they_want: Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) supplies and maintenance services for use in general rehabilitation therapy interventions.

original_title: ASC23043 - Positive Behaviour Support Supported Living Services in Herefordshire & Worcestershire DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Positive Behaviour Support Supported Living Services in Herefordshire and Worcestershire, established jointly by Worcestershire County Council and NHS Worcestershire and Herefordshire Integrated Care Board. Suppliers can apply at any time throughout the DPS term, with evaluations conducted in periodic rounds, and successful applicants will be invited to participate in future mini competitions or referrals for placements.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced, qualified and appropriately accredited suppliers who can meet selection criteria and minimum standards as requested in the SSQ Questionnaire (Stage 1) and Evaluation (Stage 2). No maximum number of suppliers.

original_title: National Dynamic Purchasing System for Mobile Diagnostic and Treatment Units
english_summary: A Dynamic Purchasing System for mobile diagnostic and treatment units to increase NHS capacity for elective procedures and reduce waiting lists. Units will be deployed on hospital sites or in community locations like supermarkets and GP practices to improve patient access to healthcare services.
what_they_want: Mobile diagnostic and/or treatment units in the form of vans or non-permanent units/cabins. Three procurement options: Lot 1 - Hire or Lease of units; Lot 2 - Purchase of units; Lot 3 - Units as a Managed Service.

original_title: Nursing Homes Preferred Provider List For Derby and Derbyshire ICB
buyer_name: NHS Derby & Derbyshire Integrated Care Board
english_summary: NHS Derby & Derbyshire Integrated Care Board is establishing a Nursing Home Preferred Provider List using an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) basis, similar to a Dynamic Purchasing System. The contract runs for 5 years with an optional 2-year extension, with providers able to apply at any time during the contract period on a zero value, zero volume basis.
what_they_want: Nursing home services to be placed on a preferred provider list for the Derby and Derbyshire Integrated Care Board area.

original_title: Nursing Homes Preferred Provider List For Derby and Derbyshire ICB
english_summary: NHS Derby & Derbyshire ICB is establishing a Nursing Home Preferred Provider List using an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) basis, similar to a Dynamic Purchasing System. Providers can apply at any time during the 5-year contract period (with optional 2-year extension), with successful applicants added to the preferred provider list on a rolling basis.
what_they_want: Nursing home services for placement of patients requiring nursing care. This is a zero value, zero volume framework agreement establishing a preferred provider list rather than procurement of specific equipment.

original_title: Adults with Disabilities Pseudo-DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for providing support services to working-age adults (16+) with disabilities and/or long-term health conditions who live within Sheffield. The services include support within the home and/or activities and support outside the home, running from March 27, 2023 to March 26, 2033.
what_they_want: Support services for adults with disabilities including in-home support and activities/support outside the home for working-age individuals (aged 16 or over) with disabilities and/or long-term health conditions.

original_title: Children and Young People Continuing Healthcare
buyer_name: NHS Arden and Greater East Midlands Commissioning Support Unit
english_summary: This is a tender for a Preferred Provider List to deliver continuing healthcare services for children and young people with ongoing care needs in Norfolk and Waveney. Services must be available 24/7, 365 days per year in accordance with individual care plans, awarded on a zero value, zero volume basis for 3 years.
what_they_want: Continuing healthcare services for children and young people with determined Continuing Care needs, available 24 hours a day, 365 days per year in Norfolk and Waveney area.

original_title: Pseudo Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) / Referral Management Services (RMS)
buyer_name: NHS NORTH OF ENGLAND COMMISSIONING SUPPORT (HOSTED BY NHS ENGLAND)
english_summary: This tender establishes a Pseudo Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Rapid Specialist Opinion and Referral Management Services on behalf of multiple NHS Clinical Commissioning Groups in North England. The system will allow participating NHS organizations to procure specialist medical opinion and referral management services over the contract term.
what_they_want: Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) services and Referral Management Services (RMS) for healthcare patient pathways and clinical decision support.

original_title: Flexible Procurement System (FPS) for the Provision of Independent Assessors and Reviewers
english_summary: City of Doncaster Council is establishing a Flexible Procurement System for independent assessors and reviewers across seven lots covering mental health assessments, safeguarding reviews, and complaints handling. This is not a medical devices procurement but rather professional services for assessment and review work in social care and safeguarding contexts.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND

original_title: Older Peoples Care Homes
english_summary: This is a tender for the provision of care home services for older people in Sheffield, focused on delivering personalized care that promotes independence, wellbeing, and quality of life while supporting the city's Health and Social Care vision.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
